seoTitle: Create Group Forms and Move Personal Forms in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Build collaborative group forms in Microsoft Forms or move an existing personal form into a shared team workspace with one click.
intro: A survey that starts as a personal project often needs to become a shared team asset once other people need to co-edit it or view live results. Microsoft Forms lets you either build directly inside a group workspace or move an existing personal form over later.

steps.heading: Open your group workspace
steps.body: From the main dashboard, scroll past your recent personal files to the My Groups panel and select the target Microsoft 365 Group or Teams workspace.

steps.heading: Create a form directly in the group
steps.body: Click New Group Quiz or New Group Form to build a shared asset straight from that group directory.

steps.heading: Or find an existing personal form
steps.body: Return to your My Forms tab and locate the private template you want to transfer.

steps.heading: Move it to a group
steps.body: Hover over the form tile, click the three-dot More Options icon, and select Move to a group, then choose your destination group.

steps.heading: Confirm the transfer
steps.body: Click Move to authorize the transfer, then open the target group folder to verify the form appears for all team members.

faq.question: What happens to responses when I move a personal form to a group?
faq.answer: Moving a form transfers ownership and preserves the response links, so existing data carries over with it.

faq.question: Can all group members edit a shared form?
faq.answer: Yes, once a form is inside a group, all group members can view incoming analytics and co-edit question parameters.

faq.question: Does moving a form change its underlying data destination?
faq.answer: Yes, group-owned forms automatically sync their Excel data to a shared SharePoint or Teams cloud drive.

faq.question: Can I move a form back to my personal account later?
faq.answer: The video focuses on moving personal forms into groups — check your organization's permissions if you need to reverse a transfer.
